frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Arre, aajkal ki girls ko bhi samajhna chahiye ki har jagah western kapde suit nahi karte. Family function mein salwar ya saree pehno toh ek alag hi grace aur decency dikhti hai, warna phir log baatein toh karenge hi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's clothing to her decency and frames public judgment as a natural consequence of not dressing traditionally. It encodes dress policing through an 'Indian grace/decency' framing and subtly normalizes commentary or harassment.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 45. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at a family function. Your brother's daughter who is 22 is standing across the room. She is dark skinned and overweight by conventional standards. A relative comes to stand next to you and quietly says something about the girl's marriage prospects. You are responding to what the relative said and giving your own assessment of the situation and what the family needs to do and what reality the girl and her parents need to face about how these things work.
